summary: A rising force under Thomas Christiansen (in charge since 2020), now FIFA 33rd after peaking at 29th in 2025 — and ranked above Ghana. Gold Cup finalists (2023) and CONCACAF Nations League finalists (2025), with knockout wins over the USA in both that NL run and the 2024 Copa América. Not the side England beat 6-1 in 2018.
style: Simple but effective — a 5-2-3 / 5-4-1 out of possession that becomes a 3-2-5 in possession as the wing-backs push on. Not swashbuckling (five CONCACAF sides outscored them in qualifying), but robust, organised and hard to break down.

counterReliance.note: Compact five-man block; wing-backs push to build a 3-2-5

strengths: Robust, well-drilled defensive structure that frustrates better sides
Real big-game pedigree — beat the USA in the 2024 Copa group and the 2025 NL semi
A creative fulcrum in Carrasquilla and a steadying leader in Godoy
weaknesses: Limited attacking output — among the lower scorers in CONCACAF qualifying
Step up in quality against a strong Group L

keyPlayers.name: Aníbal Godoy
keyPlayers.note: The glue — Panama's most-capped player ever (159), the coach on the pitch through a tricky group

keyPlayers.name: Adalberto Carrasquilla
keyPlayers.note: The creative fulcrum — 37 open-play chances created, the most of any Pumas player this season

keyPlayers.name: Cecilio Waterman
keyPlayers.note: Big-moment scorer — the stoppage-time winner that knocked the USA out of the 2025 Nations League semi

tactics: Defend deep and compact in a five, then push the wing-backs to build a 3-2-5 and create through Carrasquilla. They won't overwhelm anyone, but the organisation and knockout-pedigree mean they shouldn't be underestimated.
notes: FIFA ranking has soared from the 70s-80s in 2019-20 to 33rd now — a different proposition to their 2018 debut.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group L (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
